message.thinking:
We need to output JSON array of 3 objects.

Each object has "question": "Chapters" or "Description" or "Structure".

"feedback": text.

"complies": boolean.

We need to evaluate the provided thesis introduction final section. The three questions:

1. Chapters: Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The provided text includes a table of plan but not explicit chapter headings. The text includes "1. DARBA BŪTĪBA UN AKTUALITĀTE" then "1.1. tabula" which is a table. It doesn't list other chapters or sections. So it likely doesn't list main sections. So compliance false.

2. Description: Does the text briefly describe contents of each section. There is description of section 1, but no other sections. So it partially describes. But the question asks if text briefly describes contents of each section. There's only one section, described. That could satisfy? But maybe they expect each section described. There is only one, so it does describe. But does it list? Might be okay. I'd say it does describe contents of section 1. So compliance true? But maybe lacking description of other sections. It's ambiguous. I'd lean that it does not fully describe because no other sections. So compliance false.

3. Structure: Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? There is only one section and a table. Not enough. So false.

Thus results:

Chapters: false.

Description: false.

Structure: false.

Provide feedback each.
